essential_question: Eleven lessons from special triangles to trig ratios — can you still prove it, not just remember it?
objective: Revisit and connect the vocabulary and skills from Lessons 20–30 — special right triangles through the six trig ratios — and surface any gaps before the Unit 03 capstone build and MUS Unit Test III.

content.prompt: Sketch a right triangle. Label it with every tool from Lessons 20–30 you can still name: a ratio, a proof method, a transformation.

agenda.title: Review — Special Right Triangles & Proof Basics (L20–L22)

content.heading: L20–L22 in sixty seconds each
bullets: L21 30-60-90 Triangles — short leg : long leg : hypotenuse = 1 : √3 : 2.
L22 Postulates, Theorems & Angle Relationships — the rules every proof leans on.

content.heading: L23–L26 in sixty seconds each
bullets: L23 Congruent Triangles & Corresponding Parts — CPCTC: once triangles match, every part matches.
L26 Right-Triangle Congruence: HL, LL, HA, LA — the right-triangle-only shortcuts.

content.heading: L27–L30 in sixty seconds each
bullets: L27 Similar Triangles & AA Similarity — two congruent angle pairs force similarity, and a scale factor.
L28 Transformations — translations, reflections, rotations on the coordinate plane.
L30 The Six Trigonometric Ratios — sine, cosine, tangent, and their reciprocals.

content.heading: One from each third of the unit

content.preview: Next session: the Unit 03 capstone — a real-world trig measurement plus a transformation design, written up with a proof.

success_criteria.text: I can name the key ratios, proof methods, and transformations from Lessons 20–30
I can choose and apply the correct special-triangle ratio, congruence/similarity shortcut, or trig ratio for a given triangle
I can identify which of Lessons 20–30 I need to reteach before the test

questions.prompt: In a 45-45-90 triangle, the hypotenuse equals a leg times…
questions.explain: The 45-45-90 ratio is leg : leg : hypotenuse = 1 : 1 : √2.

questions.prompt: Which shortcut proves two right triangles congruent using only their hypotenuses and one leg?
questions.explain: HL (Hypotenuse-Leg) is a congruence shortcut that only applies to right triangles.

questions.prompt: A 30-60-90 triangle has a short leg of 7. Find the long leg and the hypotenuse.
questions.explain: The ratio is short leg : long leg : hypotenuse = 1 : √3 : 2, so multiply 7 by √3 and by 2.

questions.prompt: Two triangles have two pairs of congruent angles. What can you conclude?
questions.explain: AA similarity: two congruent angle pairs force similarity, not necessarily congruence.

questions.prompt: A right triangle has an acute angle of 32° and adjacent leg 10. Which trig ratio and equation would you use to find the opposite leg?
questions.explain: Tangent relates the opposite and adjacent legs of a right triangle: tan(angle) = opposite/adjacent.

questions.prompt: Exit: which of Lessons 20–30 do you most need to review again before the capstone and the test — name it and why.
